hypothesis: Incorporating temperature-dependent molecular conformational dynamics into foundation model pre-training—by augmenting SMILES corpora with molecular dynamics trajectory descriptors at varying thermal conditions—will significantly improve prediction accuracy for ionic conductivity in electrolyte formulations across wide temperature ranges (−40°C to 80°C), because the current static SMILES-based embeddings fail to capture thermally-induced changes in solvation shell geometry and ion-pair dissociation equilibria that govern macroscale transport properties, and this improvement will be most pronounced in low-data regimes where the thermal extrapolation gap between training and out-of-distribution test sets is largest.
